title: Slow Cooked Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty and comforting Algerian dish featuring tender lamb in a rich caramel sauce, slow cooked to perfection in a tandoor style clay pot. This dish is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay pot or slow cooker over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the caramel sauce and chicken broth, and stir to combine.
Cover the clay pot or slow cooker and transfer it to the oven or cook on low for 8 hours.
After 8 hours, remove the pot from the oven or turn off the slow cooker and let it cool slightly.
Serve the lamb and sauce over couscous or rice.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
